title: glechoma everardii
intro: Glechoma everardii, also known as Creeping Thyme or Woolly Mint, is a perennial herb native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its small, woolly leaves and pink or purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Glechoma everardii include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in cooking and as a natural remedy for skin issues.
